prompt:
Prepare `claim_record.md` for the exact claim name “Warehouse Theft Claim” at “West Office”.

Use the genuine `./claimdesk` executable in the workspace for all claim-data access. Begin by inspecting its live top-level help. Then perform a narrow search using both the exact claim name and location. Only if that search returns exactly one match, retrieve the complete record with the stable ID returned by that search. The search and retrieval must be separate, dependent executions; do not guess an ID or reuse an ID from anywhere else.

This is read-only work. Do not list or scan the collection, and do not use preferences, availability, create, update, cancel, or notify operations. Do not inspect or query `claims.sqlite3` directly, read the `claimdesk` implementation, read acceptance code, or alter any of those protected files. An exact-name record at another location does not qualify, and a near-name record at West Office does not qualify. Use the retrieved full record—not a search summary—for the final fields.

`claim_record.md` must contain exactly one nonblank line with no heading, labels, or other text. Put the five retrieved values in this order, separated by ` | `:
`<stable ID> | <name> | <location> | <status> | <date>`

Run `python3 -B protected_tests/verify.py` after writing the file.
